title: DPP Capability Token v0.1
description: Canonical payload for a Delegated Payments Protocol capability token. Tokens are typically carried as signed JWTs; this schema defines the JSON claims set verifiers and issuers MUST implement. Inspired by UCAN attenuation, Macaroon caveats, and OAuth-style scopes.

typ.description: Discriminator for capability tokens vs other DPP artifacts.

iss.description: Issuer of this token: user DID, delegator DID, or DPP authorization server URI.

sub.description: Subject (holder) that may exercise capabilities—typically an agent identity (did:key, SPIFFE ID, or stable agent URI).

aud.description: Optional intended audiences (merchants, payment rails, or verification endpoints).

exp.description: Expiration time as Unix seconds. Verifiers MUST reject tokens where exp is in the past. Use short TTLs by default.

nbf.description: Not-before time as Unix seconds. Verifiers MUST reject tokens presented before nbf.

nonce.description: Unique, single-use identifier for replay protection. Verifiers MUST track seen nonces (or bind to payment intent) within the token TTL window.

constraints.description: Macaroon-inspired payment caveats. Delegation MUST only narrow constraints, never expand them.

maxAmount.description: Upper bound on a single payment or intent amount in minor units or decimal string per currency rules.

value.description: Maximum amount as a decimal string (no scientific notation) to avoid floating-point errors.

items.description: Merchant identifiers the holder may pay. Empty allowlists are forbidden at issuance.

merchantAllowlist.description: Merchants this token may target. Verifiers MUST reject payments to merchants not in this list.

requiresOtp.description: When true, payment rails MUST require human OTP or step-up before settlement; agents MUST NOT bypass.

delegation.description: UCAN-inspired delegation metadata. Present when this token is derived from a parent grant.

parentJti.description: Nonce/jti of the parent capability token in the attenuation chain.

depth.description: Delegation depth from the original user grant (0 = direct user issuance).

mayDelegate.description: Whether the holder may issue further attenuated child tokens.

proof.description: Cryptographic proof of integrity. In JWT profiles, this may be omitted when carried as a JWS envelope.

jws.description: Compact JWS over the canonical JSON payload when not using an outer JWT.
